preview_text:
The model makes France the favorite with a 55% win probability, while Norway's chances stand at 17%. This represents a substantial gap between the two sides in the statistical forecast, positioning France as the clear frontrunner on a neutral pitch.

The 28% draw probability indicates meaningful competitive balance despite France's advantage. The spread across outcomes—54.6% France, 28.0% draw, 17.4% Norway—suggests this match is unlikely to be one-sided, with nearly three in ten outcomes resulting in a stalemate. The match has room for tactical attrition or deadlock rather than inevitable dominance.

Model and market are closely aligned. The sportsbook consensus places France at 53% and Norway at 22%, within a few percentage points of the model across all three outcomes. Polymarket prices move France slightly higher at 55% but otherwise track the statistical forecast tightly. No material divergence signals disagreement on the likely competitive character of this fixture.
